retrospective.harder_than_needed: The spec's Technical Approach and pre-authored verification.json questions fully determined the target shape (props unchanged, pubkeyHex-in adapter, entries derived live from S1's predicate, guidance-state substring constraints), so the design itself had no ambiguity. The one self-inflicted friction: my own dev-delegation prompt included an over-broad grep requirement ('zero matches for parseContactCard anywhere in the file') intended only to confirm the import/call site was removed, but it also caught the doc-comment's prose mention of the function name, and dev complied literally by rewording the comment to avoid naming parseContactCard at all. That weakened AC-STRUCT-3's 'correctly attribute the seam to parseContactCard' requirement below its clearest form. Caught on independent review and fixed with a one-line edit, but a tighter-scoped grep (matching only import/call syntax, not the bare identifier) in the original prompt would have avoided the correction round entirely.

absorbed_from.note: dev flagged three judgment calls inline (no formal RETROSPECTIVE: block): (1) reworded the parseContactCard doc-comment to satisfy an overly literal grep instruction from me, later corrected; (2) rendered the guidance state as a standalone sibling VStack rather than reusing inviteContactLabel inside a second FormControl, judged clearer; (3) skipped the optional empty-value placeholder <option> since plain value="" already renders nothing pre-selected. (2) and (3) are sound implementation choices, absorbed as-is; (1) is captured above as the harder_than_needed friction.
